---
title: "Fraud Detection & Account Flow Dashboard"
author: "FAYOMI ABIGAIL OLUDAYO"
format: 
  dashboard:
    orientation: rows
    theme: cosmo
execute:
  echo: false
warning: false
---

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px
import itables

# Load data
df = pd.read_excel("data/bank.xlsx")

df.columns = (
    df.columns.astype(str)
    .str.strip()
    .str.replace(" ", "_", regex=False)
)

# 3. DROP EXTRA COLUMN
if "." in df.columns:
    df = df.drop(columns=["."])

# 4. CLEAN DATA
df["Account_No"] = df["Account_No"].astype(str).str.replace("'", "", regex=False).str.strip()

df["TRANSACTION_DETAILS"] = (
    df["TRANSACTION_DETAILS"]
    .astype(str)
    .str.replace("\xa0", " ", regex=False)
    .str.strip()
)

df["DATE"] = pd.to_datetime(df["DATE"], errors="coerce")
df["VALUE_DATE"] = pd.to_datetime(df["VALUE_DATE"], errors="coerce")

for col in ["WITHDRAWAL_AMT", "DEPOSIT_AMT", "BALANCE_AMT"]:
    df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors="coerce").fillna(0)

df = df.dropna(subset=["DATE"]).copy()

# 5. FEATURE ENGINEERING
df["Net_Transaction"] = df["DEPOSIT_AMT"] - df["WITHDRAWAL_AMT"]
df["Transaction_Amount"] = df[["DEPOSIT_AMT", "WITHDRAWAL_AMT"]].max(axis=1)
df["Large_Transaction_Flag"] = np.where(df["Transaction_Amount"] > 500000, "Yes", "No")
df["Negative_Balance_Flag"] = np.where(df["BALANCE_AMT"] < 0, "Yes", "No")
df["Month"] = df["DATE"].dt.to_period("M").astype(str)

df = df.sort_values(["Account_No", "DATE"]).reset_index(drop=True)
df["Cumulative_Balance"] = df.groupby("Account_No")["Net_Transaction"].cumsum()

# 6. KPI VALUES
total_deposits = df["DEPOSIT_AMT"].sum()
total_withdrawals = df["WITHDRAWAL_AMT"].sum()
average_balance = df["BALANCE_AMT"].mean()
high_value_tx = (df["Large_Transaction_Flag"] == "Yes").sum()
negative_accounts = df.loc[df["BALANCE_AMT"] < 0, "Account_No"].nunique()

# 7. ACCOUNT SUMMARY
account_summary = df.groupby("Account_No", as_index=False).agg({
    "DEPOSIT_AMT": "sum",
    "WITHDRAWAL_AMT": "sum",
    "Transaction_Amount": "mean",
    "BALANCE_AMT": "mean",
    "Large_Transaction_Flag": lambda x: (x == "Yes").sum()
})

account_summary.columns = [
    "Account_No",
    "Total_Deposits",
    "Total_Withdrawals",
    "Avg_Transaction_Amount",
    "Avg_Balance",
    "High_Value_Transactions"
]

account_summary["Risk_Score"] = (
    (account_summary["Total_Withdrawals"] > account_summary["Total_Deposits"]).astype(int)
    + (account_summary["High_Value_Transactions"] > 0).astype(int)
    + (account_summary["Avg_Balance"] < 0).astype(int)
)

account_summary["Risk_Level"] = np.select(
    [
        account_summary["Risk_Score"] >= 3,
        account_summary["Risk_Score"] == 2
    ],
    [
        "High Risk",
        "Medium Risk"
    ],
    default="Low Risk"
)

# 8. MONTHLY FLOW
monthly_flow = df.groupby("Month", as_index=False).agg({
    "DEPOSIT_AMT": "sum",
    "WITHDRAWAL_AMT": "sum"
})

monthly_melted = monthly_flow.melt(
    id_vars="Month",
    value_vars=["DEPOSIT_AMT", "WITHDRAWAL_AMT"],
    var_name="Flow_Type",
    value_name="Amount"
)

# 9. TOP 10 TRANSACTIONS
top_10 = df.sort_values("Transaction_Amount", ascending=False).head(10).copy()
top_10["Label"] = top_10["Account_No"] + " | " + top_10["DATE"].dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")

# 10. VISUALS
# Top 5 risky accounts
top_accounts = account_summary.sort_values("Risk_Score", ascending=False)["Account_No"].head(5)

filtered_df = df[df["Account_No"].isin(top_accounts)]

fig = px.line(
    filtered_df,
    x="DATE",
    y="BALANCE_AMT",
    color="Account_No",
    title="Balance Trend (Top Risk Accounts)",
    markers=True,
    color_discrete_sequence=px.colors.qualitative.Bold
)

# Balance Trends by Account
account_melted = account_summary.melt(
    id_vars="Account_No",
    value_vars=["Total_Deposits", "Total_Withdrawals"],
    var_name="Transaction_Type",
    value_name="Amount"
)

fig_bar = px.bar(
    account_melted,
    x="Account_No",
    y="Amount",
    color="Transaction_Type",
    title="Deposits vs Withdrawals per Account",
    barmode="group",
    color_discrete_sequence=px.colors.qualitative.Set2
).update_layout(
    template="plotly_white",
    title_x=0.5,
    xaxis_title="Account Number",
    yaxis_title="Amount"
)

# Monthly Inflow vs Outflow
fig_stacked = px.bar(
    monthly_melted,
    x="Month",
    y="Amount",
    color="Flow_Type",
    title="Monthly Inflow vs Outflow",
    barmode="stack",
    color_discrete_sequence=px.colors.qualitative.Pastel
)

# Deposit vs Withdrawal (Outlier Detection)
threshold_99 = df["Transaction_Amount"].quantile(0.99)

df["Top_1pct_Anomaly"] = np.where(
    df["Transaction_Amount"] >= threshold_99,
    "Top 1% Anomaly",
    "Normal"
)

deposit_threshold = df["DEPOSIT_AMT"].quantile(0.95)
withdrawal_threshold = df["WITHDRAWAL_AMT"].quantile(0.95)

fig_scatter = px.scatter(
    df,
    x="DEPOSIT_AMT",
    y="WITHDRAWAL_AMT",
    color="Top_1pct_Anomaly",
    size="Transaction_Amount",
    hover_data=["Account_No", "DATE", "TRANSACTION_DETAILS", "BALANCE_AMT"],
    title="Deposit vs Withdrawal (Top 1% Anomalies)",
    color_discrete_map={
        "Normal": "#4C78A8",
        "Top 1% Anomaly": "#E63946"
    }
).update_layout(
    template="plotly_white",
    title_x=0.5,
    xaxis_title="Deposit Amount ($)",
    yaxis_title="Withdrawal Amount ($)"
)

# Show all figures
fig_top10 = px.bar(
    top_10.sort_values("Transaction_Amount"),
    x="Transaction_Amount",
    y="Label",
    orientation="h",
    color="Transaction_Amount",
    title="Top 10 Largest Transactions",
    color_continuous_scale=px.colors.sequential.Viridis
)

fig_risk = px.bar(
    account_summary.sort_values("Risk_Score", ascending=False),
    x="Account_No",
    y="Risk_Score",
    color="Risk_Level",
    title="Risk Score by Account",
    color_discrete_sequence=px.colors.qualitative.Set1
)
def format_currency(value):
    if value >= 1_000_000_000:
        return f"${value/1_000_000_000:.1f}B"
    elif value >= 1_000_000:
        return f"${value/1_000_000:.1f}M"
    else:
        return f"${value:,.0f}"
```


# OVERVIEW
This dashboard analyzes bank transaction activity to uncover unusual account behavior, identify high-value transactions, and monitor the flow of deposits and withdrawals over time. 

It highlights potential fraud signals by combining transaction trends, account-level summaries, and rule-based risk scoring.

## Row {height="20%"}

::: {.valuebox icon="cash-stack" color="#2a9d8f" title="Total Deposits"}
`{python} format_currency(total_deposits)`
:::

::: {.valuebox icon="cash-coin" color="#e76f51" title="Total Withdrawals"}
`{python} format_currency(total_withdrawals)`
:::

::: {.valuebox icon="bank" color="#457b9d" title="Average Balance"}
`{python} format_currency(average_balance)`
:::

::: {.valuebox icon="exclamation-triangle-fill" color="#dda15e" title="High Value Transactions"}
`{python} f"{high_value_tx}"`
:::

::: {.valuebox icon="dash-circle-fill" color="#885A89" title="Negative Balance Accounts"}
`{python} f"{negative_accounts}"`
:::


## Row {height="80%"}

### Column {width="50%"}

```{python}
fig.show()
```

This chart focuses on the top risk accounts and tracks how their balances change over time.
Some accounts show sharp drops and unstable balance movement, which may indicate unusually large withdrawals or rapid movement of funds.

By filtering to only the riskiest accounts, the chart becomes more useful for investigation and helps surface accounts that deserve closer attention.

### Column {width="50%"}

```{python}
fig_risk.show()
```

This chart ranks accounts based on a simple rule-based risk score.
Accounts are scored higher when they show warning signs such as:

withdrawals greater than deposits
presence of high-value transactions
weak or negative average balances

It helps prioritize which accounts should be reviewed first during fraud investigation.

# ACCOUNT ACTIVITY AND CASH FLOW

## Row {height="50%"}

### Column {width="50%"}

```{python}
fig_bar.show()
```

This comparison of total deposits and withdrawals per account shows how money moves in and out of each account.
Accounts where withdrawals are consistently higher than deposits may indicate unusual outflow behavior, especially when combined with other fraud signals.

The chart also helps distinguish between accounts that mainly accumulate funds and those that rapidly deplete them.

### Column {width="50%"}

```{python}
fig_stacked.show()
```

The monthly inflow and outflow chart highlights how transaction volumes evolve over time.
Periods with sudden spikes in deposits or withdrawals can point to abnormal activity, coordinated transfers, or unusual cash movement.

This view is useful for spotting months that deserve deeper investigation.


# TRANSACTION ANOMALIES

## Row {height="50%"}

### Column {width="50%"}

```{python}
fig_scatter.show()
```

By isolating the top 1% of transactions, the chart clearly highlights high-value anomalies that were previously hidden. 

These anomalies are concentrated in accounts showing either large withdrawals without deposits or large deposits without corresponding outflows, indicating potentially suspicious or non-standard financial behavior.


### Column {width="50%"}

```{python}
fig_top10.show()
```

This chart isolates the 10 largest transactions in the dataset.
A concentration of very large transactions within specific accounts may suggest structured movement of funds or unusually active accounts.

It provides a quick way to identify the transactions that deserve immediate review.

# SUMMARY

This dashboard transforms raw transaction records into a fraud-focused monitoring view. It combines account balance behavior, transaction flow, anomaly detection, and risk scoring to highlight where investigators should focus first.

The analysis shows that not all accounts behave the same way. Some accounts demonstrate unstable balance trends, some withdraw more than they deposit, and others are associated with repeated large-value transactions. These patterns do not prove fraud on their own, but they do provide strong indicators of elevated financial risk.

Overall, the dashboard improves visibility into account activity and helps surface the patterns that matter most for fraud detection and account monitoring.

## DATA DOWNLOAD

```{python}
itables.show(
    df,
    caption="Bank Transactions Dataset",
    buttons=["csvHtml5"]
)
```
